calibration_note: v1.4 adds 9 signals from Horizon calibration: org structure, spend economics, customer concentration, multi-cloud migration, tech debt itemization, tooling landscape, product portfolio, data incidents, hosting costs.

signals.name: Cyclomatic Complexity
signals.technical_definition: A measure of the number of linearly independent paths through a program's source code.
signals.primary_data_sources: Static Code Analysis Tools (e.g., SonarQube)
signals.contextual_modifiers: Codebase Age, Language, Team Skill Level
signals.interpretation_guidance: High complexity indicates code that is difficult to test and maintain, increasing the risk of defects.

signals.name: Cloud-Native Architecture Adoption
signals.technical_definition: The percentage of the application portfolio that is built using cloud-native principles.
signals.primary_data_sources: Cloud Provider Bills, Container Orchestration Platform Configurations, Architecture Review
signals.contextual_modifiers: Scalability Needs, Cost Optimization Goals, Speed of Innovation
signals.interpretation_guidance: High adoption indicates a modern, scalable, and resilient architecture.

signals.name: Technology Stack Tenancy
signals.technical_definition: The degree to which the technology stack supports multi-tenant or single-tenant deployment models.
signals.primary_data_sources: CMDB, Architecture Diagrams, Developer Surveys
signals.contextual_modifiers: Company Size, M&A History, SaaS vs. On-Premise Model
signals.interpretation_guidance: A well-defined tenancy model reduces operational complexity and supports scalable growth.

signals.name: Technical Debt Remediation Cost
signals.technical_definition: The estimated cost to remediate the existing technical debt.
signals.primary_data_sources: Static Code Analysis Tools, Architecture Review
signals.contextual_modifiers: Growth Strategy, M&A Plans, Product Roadmap
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high cost can be a major post-acquisition investment.

signals.name: Platform Consolidation Opportunity
signals.technical_definition: An assessment of the potential to consolidate duplicate or overlapping technology platforms.
signals.primary_data_sources: CMDB, Application Portfolio Management Tools, Vendor Contracts
signals.contextual_modifiers: M&A History, IT Governance, Business Process Complexity
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high opportunity score indicates potential for significant cost savings.

signals.name: Open Source Software (OSS) License Risk
signals.technical_definition: The percentage of OSS components with restrictive or high-risk licenses.
signals.primary_data_sources: Software Composition Analysis (SCA) Tools
signals.contextual_modifiers: Business Model, Legal Counsel, M&A Strategy
signals.interpretation_guidance: High-risk licenses can create legal and IP complications.

signals.name: Security Framework Adoption
signals.technical_definition: The level of adoption and compliance with a recognized cybersecurity framework.
signals.primary_data_sources: Audit Reports, GRC Tools, CISO Interviews
signals.contextual_modifiers: Industry, Regulatory Requirements, Customer Contracts
signals.interpretation_guidance: Lack of a framework indicates an ad-hoc and immature security program.

signals.name: IAM Maturity
signals.technical_definition: An assessment of the maturity of IAM processes, including MFA adoption and PAM.
signals.primary_data_sources: IAM System Configurations, CISO Interviews
signals.contextual_modifiers: Company Size, Remote Workforce, Data Sensitivity
signals.interpretation_guidance: Immature IAM is a leading cause of security breaches.

signals.name: Penetration Test Findings
signals.technical_definition: The number and severity of findings from recent third-party penetration tests.
signals.primary_data_sources: Penetration Test Reports
signals.contextual_modifiers: Scope of Test, Time Since Last Test, Remediation Status
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high number of critical findings is a strong indicator of a vulnerable system.

signals.name: Vendor Security Incident Rate
signals.technical_definition: The number of security incidents that have originated from third-party vendors.
signals.primary_data_sources: SIEM, Incident Response Reports, VRM Platform
signals.contextual_modifiers: Vendor Ecosystem, Data Sharing Practices, Industry
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high rate indicates weak vendor security posture.

signals.name: Key Person Dependency
signals.technical_definition: The number of team members who would need to be unavailable before the project becomes critically compromised.
signals.primary_data_sources: HR Records, Team Structure, Code Ownership Analysis
signals.contextual_modifiers: Documentation Culture, Cross-Training Programs
signals.interpretation_guidance: A low bus factor (1 or 2) is a major operational risk.

signals.name: Engineering Attrition Rate
signals.technical_definition: The percentage of engineering staff who have left the organization over a given period.
signals.primary_data_sources: HR Records, Exit Interviews
signals.contextual_modifiers: Industry Average, Company Growth Rate, Compensation
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high attrition rate signals cultural or compensation issues.

signals.name: Glassdoor Rating
signals.technical_definition: The average Glassdoor rating for engineering roles.
signals.primary_data_sources: Glassdoor, Blind, LinkedIn
signals.contextual_modifiers: Number of Reviews, Recency, Industry Comparison
signals.interpretation_guidance: A low rating can indicate systemic issues with culture or management.

signals.name: Manager to IC Ratio
signals.technical_definition: The ratio of engineering managers to individual contributors.
signals.primary_data_sources: HR Records, Org Chart
signals.contextual_modifiers: Company Size, Team Structure, Product Complexity
signals.interpretation_guidance: An imbalanced ratio can indicate top-heavy management or insufficient oversight.

signals.name: Data Quality Score
signals.technical_definition: A composite score measuring accuracy, completeness, consistency, and timeliness of critical data assets.
signals.primary_data_sources: Data Quality Tools, Data Catalogs
signals.contextual_modifiers: Data Volume, Business Criticality, Regulatory Requirements
signals.interpretation_guidance: Low data quality undermines analytics, AI, and decision-making.

signals.name: AI Use Case Alignment
signals.technical_definition: An assessment of how well AI initiatives align with core business strategy.
signals.primary_data_sources: AI Strategy Documents, Product Roadmap, Executive Interviews
signals.contextual_modifiers: Industry AI Adoption Rate, Available Data Assets
signals.interpretation_guidance: Misaligned AI initiatives waste resources.

signals.name: AI Infrastructure Readiness
signals.technical_definition: An assessment of infrastructure capabilities to support AI/ML workloads.
signals.primary_data_sources: Cloud Bills, MLOps Platform Configuration, Architecture Review
signals.contextual_modifiers: AI Ambition, Data Volume, Model Complexity
signals.interpretation_guidance: Lack of AI infrastructure readiness will delay AI-driven value.

signals.name: IT Spend as % of Revenue
signals.technical_definition: Total IT spending as a percentage of total company revenue.
signals.primary_data_sources: Financial Statements, IT Budget
signals.contextual_modifiers: Industry Benchmarks, Growth Stage
signals.interpretation_guidance: Spending significantly above or below benchmarks warrants investigation.

signals.name: Cloud Spend Efficiency
signals.technical_definition: How effectively the company utilizes cloud spending.
signals.primary_data_sources: Cloud Provider Bills, FinOps Tools
signals.contextual_modifiers: Growth Rate, Architecture, Reserved Instance Coverage
signals.interpretation_guidance: Low efficiency indicates waste and optimization opportunities.

signals.name: COGS Technology
signals.technical_definition: The direct technology costs associated with delivering the product.
signals.primary_data_sources: Financial Statements, Cloud Bills, Vendor Contracts
signals.contextual_modifiers: Revenue Growth, Pricing Model, CAC
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high COGS ratio can compress margins.

signals.name: Innovation vs Maintenance Ratio
signals.technical_definition: The ratio of engineering effort on new features versus maintaining existing systems.
signals.primary_data_sources: Jira, Time Tracking, Sprint Data
signals.contextual_modifiers: Product Maturity, Technical Debt, Competitive Pressure
signals.interpretation_guidance: A low innovation ratio (<20%) suggests team is overwhelmed by maintenance.

signals.name: R&D Cost as % of Revenue
signals.technical_definition: Total R&D technology spend as a percentage of revenue.
signals.primary_data_sources: Financial Statements, R&D Budget
signals.contextual_modifiers: Industry Benchmarks, Growth Stage
signals.interpretation_guidance: Helps benchmark R&D efficiency against industry peers.

signals.name: CI/CD Pipeline Maturity
signals.technical_definition: An assessment of CI/CD pipeline maturity including automation level and test coverage.
signals.primary_data_sources: CI/CD Platform Configuration, Developer Surveys
signals.contextual_modifiers: Team Size, Deployment Targets, Regulatory Constraints
signals.interpretation_guidance: A mature pipeline enables faster, safer deployments.

signals.name: Deployment Frequency
signals.technical_definition: How often the organization deploys code to production.
signals.primary_data_sources: CI/CD Platform Logs, DORA Metrics
signals.contextual_modifiers: Team Size, Release Strategy
signals.interpretation_guidance: High frequency is a key indicator of a high-performing team.

signals.name: Mean Time to Recovery
signals.technical_definition: The average time to recover from a production failure.
signals.primary_data_sources: Incident Management Tools, DORA Metrics
signals.contextual_modifiers: System Complexity, On-Call Structure
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high MTTR indicates poor incident response.

signals.name: Uptime & Availability
signals.technical_definition: Actual uptime compared to stated SLOs/SLAs.
signals.primary_data_sources: Monitoring Tools, SLA Reports
signals.contextual_modifiers: Customer Contracts, Industry Standards
signals.interpretation_guidance: Consistently missed SLOs indicate operational issues.

signals.name: Disaster Recovery Readiness
signals.technical_definition: Ability to recover from a major disaster, including existence and testing of a DR plan.
signals.primary_data_sources: DR Plans, DR Test Results, BCP Documents
signals.contextual_modifiers: RPO/RTO Requirements, Regulatory Requirements
signals.interpretation_guidance: An untested or absent DR plan is a significant operational risk.

signals.name: Agile Maturity
signals.technical_definition: An assessment of Agile development process maturity.
signals.primary_data_sources: Jira, Sprint Reports, Retrospective Notes
signals.contextual_modifiers: Team Size, Distributed Teams, Organizational Culture
signals.interpretation_guidance: Low agile maturity correlates with unpredictable delivery.

signals.name: Manual vs Automated Processes
signals.technical_definition: The ratio of manual to automated processes in the SDLC.
signals.primary_data_sources: CI/CD Configuration, Test Automation Reports
signals.contextual_modifiers: Team Size, Product Complexity
signals.interpretation_guidance: A high manual ratio indicates inefficiency and higher error risk.

signals.name: Organization Structure & Leadership Alignment
signals.technical_definition: Assessment of reporting structure efficiency, span of control, and leadership alignment across technology functions.
signals.primary_data_sources: Org Charts, Role Descriptions, Leadership Bios, HR Documents
signals.contextual_modifiers: Company Size, M&A History, Growth Stage
signals.interpretation_guidance: Fragmented tech leadership signals strategic misalignment. Flag when >4 tech leaders report directly to CEO.

signals.name: R&D Spend as Percentage of Revenue
signals.technical_definition: Total R&D labor + tooling + infrastructure spend as a percentage of revenue or CARR.
signals.primary_data_sources: Financial Statements, Budget Spreadsheets, Salary Data, Cloud Bills
signals.contextual_modifiers: Growth Stage, Industry Benchmarks (SaaS: 15-25% typical)
signals.interpretation_guidance: Below 15% for growth-stage SaaS = under-investment. Above 30% = efficiency concern.

signals.name: Customer Revenue Concentration Risk
signals.technical_definition: Revenue concentration among top customers, especially on legacy platforms.
signals.primary_data_sources: Revenue Reports, Customer Lists, Contract Data
signals.contextual_modifiers: Contract Length, Switching Costs, Platform Migration Status
signals.interpretation_guidance: Any customer >10% of revenue on a legacy platform is a compound risk.

signals.name: Multi-Cloud Consolidation & Migration Risk
signals.technical_definition: Risks of operating across multiple cloud providers or hybrid environments.
signals.primary_data_sources: Architecture Diagrams, Cloud Bills, Migration Plans
signals.contextual_modifiers: Number of Customer Integrations, Data Residency Requirements
signals.interpretation_guidance: Planned consolidation with hundreds of customer interfaces is a material execution risk.

signals.name: Tech Debt Remediation Itemization
signals.technical_definition: Detailed breakdown of specific technical debt items with estimated remediation cost and timeline.
signals.primary_data_sources: Architecture Reviews, Tech Debt Registers, Roadmap Documents
signals.contextual_modifiers: Team Capacity, Competing Priorities, Revenue Impact
signals.interpretation_guidance: Extract EACH item separately with any cost or timeline estimates. Aggregate into total.

signals.name: Development & Enterprise Tooling Landscape
signals.technical_definition: Inventory of development, enterprise, and security tools in use.
signals.primary_data_sources: IT Asset Inventories, Tool Subscription Lists, Vendor Contracts
signals.contextual_modifiers: Company Size, Tool Consolidation Opportunity
signals.interpretation_guidance: Look for critical gaps (no HRIS, no SIEM, no code scanning), tool sprawl, AI tool adoption.

signals.name: Product Portfolio & Market Position
signals.technical_definition: Product portfolio assessment including products, markets, retention, and technology platform per product.
signals.primary_data_sources: Product Overview Documents, Sales Decks, Retention Reports
signals.contextual_modifiers: M&A History, Market Segment, Customer Count
signals.interpretation_guidance: Flag when products run on different stacks. High retention (>90%) is GREEN.

signals.name: Data Breach & Incident History
signals.technical_definition: Historical record of data breaches, PHI/PII incidents, and detection timelines.
signals.primary_data_sources: Incident Reports, Security Logs, Breach Notification Records
signals.contextual_modifiers: Industry, Regulatory Regime, Customer Data Volume
signals.interpretation_guidance: Any PHI breach in healthcare is material. Detection delay >30 days = critical.

signals.name: Hosting Cost Breakdown & Growth Trajectory
signals.technical_definition: Provider-level hosting cost breakdown with monthly trend data.
signals.primary_data_sources: Cloud Bills, Data Center Invoices, FinOps Reports
signals.contextual_modifiers: Customer Growth Rate, Migration Status, Reserved Instance Coverage
signals.interpretation_guidance: Flag >50% month-over-month volatility, linear cost scaling, stranded data center costs.
